sections.paragraph:
μνεία, ας, ἡ [μιμνῄσκω] ‘a coming to mind’ or ‘recalling past experience or association’, in NT always as recollection of a valued pers.

—a. w. focus on one’s personal processing of recollection,memory Ro 12:13 v.l.; w. ἔχω 1 Th 3:6; 2 Ti 1:3.

—b. w. focus on memorial expression or on attention brought about in another through reference mention Phil 1: 3; w. ποιεῖςϑαι Ro 1:9; Eph 1:16; 1 Th 1:2; Phlm 4.
